facts.text: Naples was founded in 1886 by former Confederate general and Kentucky U.S. politician, marking the start of its development as a seaside community.
The Naples Zoo at Caribbean Gardens opened in 1919, becoming one of the oldest zoos on Florida’s Gulf Coast.
Passenger train service to Naples ended in May 1971, and the former station now houses the Naples Depot Museum on the National Register.
The Seaboard Air Line Railroad reached Naples in 1927, followed by the Tamiami Trail in 1928, linking the city to Miami.
Naples Historical Society preserves local heritage through Historic Palm Cottage, where visitors stroll winding brick pathways and learn about the city’s past.
Two semi‑pro soccer teams, Naples United FC (2017) and Naples City FC (2020), compete in national leagues, reflecting the city’s growing sports culture.
